feedback.text: While I applaud this act and the spirit behind it I feel it fails to cover a key aspect on digital goods and services, namely video games. Too often video game publishers sell games on Steam which either are no longer playable due to technical issues they refuse to fix or they simply arbitrarily decide to make the game unplayable after a period of time. Video games are one of the biggest digital goods markets in the EU, netting 84,4 billion euros in 2024 alone. As such I propose that this Act also include some form of stipulation requiring developers or publishers to leave the game in a playable state, for the platform it was released on, when they decide to end support for it.
